sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal. Arab. Conj. III. to quarrel with any one, especially in dice, drinking, or in dividing an inheritance. So the Hebrew-

Hithpael, to become angry, irritated (in strife). Pro 20:3, “it is an honour to a man to leave off strife, וְכָל־אֱוִיל יִתְגַּלַּע but every fool becomes angry.” Followed by בְּ of the thing, Pro 18:1. It is also used of strife itself, as becoming warmer, Pro 17:14.
